staticTable.caption: Estimated SIP maturity values at 12% annual return, 10-year tenure

examples.heading: Salaried professional, Mumbai — ₹10,000/month, 25 years
examples.text: A 30-year-old starting a ₹10,000/month SIP in a large-cap index fund at an assumed 12% annual return for 25 years accumulates approximately ₹1.89 crore at maturity, having invested only ₹30 lakhs. Compounding over 25 years generates over ₹1.59 crore in returns alone.

examples.heading: Early career, Tier 2 city — ₹2,000/month, 20 years
examples.text: Starting small is still powerful. A ₹2,000/month SIP at 12% for 20 years grows to approximately ₹19.8 lakhs on a total investment of ₹4.8 lakhs — a 4x return on invested capital.

examples.heading: 10 years vs 20 years (same ₹5,000/month at 12%)
examples.text: Doubling the investment period from 10 to 20 years — while keeping the monthly SIP identical — multiplies the final wealth by 4.3x. Total invested doubles, but returns grow by 7x.

decisionGuide.question: How much should you invest in SIP each month?
decisionGuide.answer: A common guideline is the 50-30-20 rule: allocate 20% of your monthly take-home salary toward investments. For a ₹50,000/month salary, that is ₹10,000 — split between SIP, PPF, and emergency fund depending on your existing coverage.

decisionGuide.question: Which return rate should you use?
decisionGuide.answer: Use 10–12% for large-cap or index funds (conservative), 12–15% for mid-cap funds (moderate), and no more than 15% for any long-term projection regardless of fund type. Past returns do not guarantee future performance.

decisionGuide.question: When should you increase your SIP amount?
decisionGuide.answer: Increase your SIP amount by 10% each year as your income grows — this is called a step-up SIP. Use our Step-Up SIP Calculator to see how this compounds your final returns significantly.

faq.q: What is the return on a ₹5,000 SIP for 10 years?
faq.a: A ₹5,000/month SIP at 12% annual return over 10 years grows to approximately ₹11.6 lakhs. Your total investment is ₹6 lakhs and the estimated returns are approximately ₹5.6 lakhs. At a more conservative 10% return, the maturity value is approximately ₹10.3 lakhs.

faq.q: Is SIP better than a fixed deposit in India?
faq.a: SIP in equity mutual funds historically outperforms fixed deposits over 10+ year horizons. FDs offer guaranteed returns (currently 6.5–7.5% in 2026) with no risk, while equity SIP carries market risk but has delivered 11–14% annual returns over most 10-year periods. SIP suits long-term goals; FD suits short-term safety.

faq.q: What is the minimum amount for a SIP in India?
faq.a: Most mutual fund houses in India allow SIPs starting from ₹500 per month. Some funds accept ₹100/month for micro-SIP schemes. There is no maximum limit — you can invest any amount depending on your financial goals.

faq.q: Can I stop or pause a SIP anytime?
faq.a: Yes. SIPs in India can be paused or stopped at any time without penalty. You can also reduce or increase the SIP amount. Stopping a SIP does not redeem your existing investment — the units you hold remain invested and continue to grow.

The SIP calculator uses the future value of a recurring investment formula: M = P × [{(1 + r)^n − 1} / r] × (1 + r), where P is the monthly SIP, r is the monthly return rate (annual rate ÷ 12 ÷ 100), and n is the total number of months.

faq.q: What is the best SIP duration for maximum returns?
faq.a: Longer durations always produce higher returns due to compounding. A 20-year SIP generates 4–5x more wealth than a 10-year SIP at the same monthly amount and return rate. Start as early as possible and stay invested through market cycles for best results.

related.desc: Calculate returns with annual SIP amount increases

howToSteps.name: Enter Monthly Investment
howToSteps.text: Enter the amount you plan to invest every month. The minimum SIP is ₹500.

howToSteps.name: Set Expected Return Rate
howToSteps.text: Enter the expected annual return rate. Use 10–12% for conservative large-cap or index fund estimates.

howToSteps.name: Choose Investment Duration
howToSteps.text: Select how many years you plan to continue the SIP. Longer duration increases returns significantly due to compounding.

howToSteps.name: Read Your Results
howToSteps.text: The calculator shows your total invested amount, estimated returns earned, and final maturity value instantly.

schema.appDescription: Calculate SIP returns, maturity value and year-by-year growth for Indian mutual fund investments
